title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Or Dagan: Optimizing Accuracy, Cost, and Latency in Real-World Agents

So this is what we do. uh uh with A21 Maestro and this is our approach. Uh it's basically two parts. The first part is offline at build time. So at build time we're trying to find that optimal uh portfolio that I just mentioned optimal uh amount of models, agents, tools that gives us the best action space we call it. and we want to sample that in an efficient way so it doesn't cost a lot. Using that we train what we call an action model. That action model is tasked to uh predict the accuracy cost and latency of a new action. So given a task in browse comp what would cost me GBD5 with latent interaction and what would be the accuracy of that? Once we have that action model at inference, we plug it into our runtime. The runtime is budget aware. It always collects the data of everything that runs and it uses those predictions from the action model to dynamically execute and orchestrate different paths. So if I have more budget, I can use more. If I have a new task coming in, I can use the right tools at the right time. The efficient part of uh running the optimization is important. So we sample the space in a smart way. Uh we don't really have to produce all the rollouts for every point in the space. We only have to have enough to have some confidence of our predictions, right? We call that uh anytime fashion generation because at any time you have the best candidate to this point to this latency constraint or to this budget constraints. If it's important enough, put more time into it, put more money into it and get a better result.
